ab: isa 2022 wa kawash, zing-huu pishfazaq miniahala inai a thau sa thau a tnuran shduuiza muqayza matash sa thau a qbit a lhanaz.
ch: 在2022年，政府召開了原住民身分證可單列族名的會議。
en: In 2022, the government announced that indigenous identification cards can include tribal names separately.

ab: iaku makikalhi sa mathuaw munsai zing-huu thmaduq mindaaq sa thau a tnuran muqayza matash sa thau a qbit a lhanaz kataunan a parhaway Panu.
ch: 我訪問了長期向政府爭取身分證「單列族名」的部落青年Panu。
en: I interviewed Panu, a young tribal member who has long been advocating for the inclusion of tribal names on identification cards.

ab: amatilhduu iaku thithu inai pangqaiza wa qali mutusi thithu a taun makikalhi thithu.
ch: 我和他約定好這個假日到他家訪問他。
en: We had arranged to visit his home this holiday for the interview.

ab: makikalhi thithu tu isisua, thithu pishfazaq iakin miakuza thithu a lhanaz minakanai, numa kmathu iakin mani mafazaq sa thithu a taun a thau, numa thithu a kataunan.
ch: 在訪問的過程中，他向我介紹了他的名字的由來，也帶我認識了他的家人、他的部落。
en: During the interview, he introduced to me the origin of his name and also introduced me to his family and tribe.

ab: makikalhi iaku thithu minu amingqtha sa lhanaz, sasaz a pinatash antu tauduu qa?
ch: 我問他為什麼堅持要改名，舊的登記方式不好嗎?
en: I asked him why he insisted on changing his name. Wasn’t the old registration method sufficient?

ab: thithu muntazaikin mzai, maqa pasain Shpuut a matash antu pubalabalay pishfazaq thithu a lhanaz, suma wa thau amalalawa thithu mzai “Pa shinshii.” numawan kafazaan suma.
ch: 他對我說，因為用中文字並不能準確的傳達他的名字，其他人還會叫他「巴先生」，也因此鬧出不少笑話。
en: He told me that using Chinese characters could not accurately convey his name, and others would still call him \"Ba Xian Sheng,\" which resulted in many jokes being made.

ab: Panu mzai, “miniahala inai a thau matash a lhanaz shunda sa naam a lhanaz, naak a lhanaz mia’azu naam a thau a qbit, naam a taun a qbit, naam a tnuran, mangqtu ianan manasha sa thau a qbit apushuqish ananak a lhanaz.”
ch: Panu說：「原住民語書寫的名字才是我們真正的名字。我的名字代表了我們的族群、我們的家族、我們的身分，希望能有更多族人找回自己的名字。」
en: Panu said, \"The names written in indigenous languages are our true names. My name represents our ethnic group, our family, our identity. I hope more fellow tribespeople can reclaim their own names.\"
